title: Cassebeera microphylla
taxonomy: Cassebeera microphylla is classified within the family Icacinaceae. This family is a part of the order Icacinales. The Icacinales order is an ancient lineage of flowering plants. Cassebeera microphylla is a plant species found in tropical regions. It is a member of the Icacinales order, which is a distinct group of flowering plants.
uses: Cassebeera microphylla, a plant native to the Himalayan region, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. In addition, Cassebeera microphylla is used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.
